admissibility.summary: FORMAL RATIONAL CALCULUS ONLY: RationalExpr syntactic differentiation/substitution identities over ℚ. Does not establish HasDerivAt, HasDerivWithinAt, continuity, existence/uniqueness, or Mathlib ODE semantics.
requiredAssumptions: explicit nonzero domainConditions covering every division denominator
candidate validity never implies completeness or uniqueness

knownLimitations: FORMAL RATIONAL CALCULUS: not HasDerivAt / analytic ODE.
NOT analytic: does not prove HasDerivAt / HasDerivWithinAt / function equality on a set (docs/security/KNOWN_TRUST_GAPS.md).
Public registry ID is algebra.formal_rational_calculus; legacy schemas and fixtures may still use symbolic_calculus/calculus path names.
Filesystem rename is deferred to avoid broken imports; MathEvidence.IR.FormalRationalCalculus is an additive alias barrel over MathEvidence.IR.CalculusExpr.
Derivative/antiderivative/ODE/recurrence checks are formal rational identities only.
Antiderivative acceptance does not claim completeness of antiderivatives.
ODE acceptance does not claim uniqueness of solutions.
Recurrence acceptance does not claim the recurrence uniquely determines the sequence.
Branch and singularity conditions must be listed explicitly; empty only when there are no divisions.
Mathematica live derivative/antiderivative requires MATHEVIDENCE_WOLFRAMSCRIPT; CI without Wolfram uses offline fixtures. ODE/recurrence live path echoes request fields for Lean check only.
